title: Holdout Protection Loop
summary: Protects evaluation integrity by detecting leakage, overfitting, and moving goalposts.
steps: Classify cases into development, validation, holdout, and must-pass sets with explicit access rules.
Check whether any holdout answer, rubric detail, or solution pattern leaked into prompts, docs, logs, or training data.
Quarantine contaminated cases and create a new versioned evaluation if needed.
Require promotion decisions to report scores by split and identify possible overfitting.
Record who can view or modify each split.
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Holdout Protection Loop for [eval_dataset, training_or_tuning_inputs, access_policy].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Classify cases into development, validation, holdout, and must-pass sets with explicit access rules.
- Check whether any holdout answer, rubric detail, or solution pattern leaked into prompts, docs, logs, or training data.
- Quarantine contaminated cases and create a new versioned evaluation if needed.
- Require promotion decisions to report scores by split and identify possible overfitting.
- Record who can view or modify each split.

Quality gates before completion:
- splits are versioned
- leakage scan completed
- contaminated cases quarantined
- score report separates splits

Stop when one of these is true:
- evaluation integrity is acceptable
- new holdout set is required
- owner accepts documented contamination risk

Finish with:
- split manifest
- leakage findings
- quarantine log
- score report

Escalate for human approval before: unquarantining leaked cases, changing promotion criteria.
